thag13.1:1.1: Who once was highly exalted in the state,
thag13.1:1.2: having gone into the service of the king of Aṅga,
thag13.1:2.1: Five should be cut, five abandoned,
thag13.1:2.4: a monk is said to be one who’s crossed over.
thag13.1:3.1: For a monk who is insolent, heedless,
thag13.1:4.1: because what should be done is cast aside,
thag13.1:4.2: what shouldn’t be done is done.
thag13.1:4.3: For those who are insolent, heedless,
thag13.1:5.1: But those whose mindfulness immersed in the body
thag13.1:5.3: —who don’t engage in what shouldn’t be done,
thag13.1:5.4: who persevere in what should be done,
thag13.1:5.6: their effluents go to their end.
thag13.1:6.1: Go by the straight path pointed out,
thag13.1:7.2: the Teacher unexcelled in the world, the One with Eyes,
thag13.1:10.3: seeing the arising of the sense media,
thag13.1:11.3: There’s nothing to be done, nothing to add
thag13.1:12.1: As a single mass of rock isn’t moved by the wind,
thag13.1:12.3: even so all forms, flavors, sounds,
thag13.1:13.2: have no effect on one who is Such.
